summary: Recent updates to the Media Capabilities in Firefox primarily focus on improving support for WebRTC, the technology that enables real-time video and audio communication over the internet. Notably, these changes include adding allowlists for legacy and WebRTC types, enabling more flexible handling of media types to enhance compatibility. Additionally, the system now includes checks for hardware acceleration, which can help improve performance for encoding and decoding audio and video. **These enhancements are crucial for ensuring smoother video calls and better overall media performance, particularly as online communication continues to grow in importance.**
